messages.author: Buzzard
messages.text: Those usually have a maximum power of 20dbm or less so it might not be worth using them as you'd have less power emitted, plus 2-3db insertion loss. But might help with Rx. Something like these https://acasom.com/collections/helium could handle full output power, with less insertion loss but perhaps a bit big for a solar node. Could always try a different antenna - I use https://paradar.co.uk/products/868mhz-5dbi-indoor-whip for my node - I THINK the out of band noise rejection is pretty good. Hopefully with enough self-amalgamting tape and/or a cable gland it would stay dry. I get a noise floor of about -114 at home with it the whip, close to the town centre. When I get a chance I might head down close to 3Shires with my testing repeater and the paradar and see what figures I get

messages.text: Well, if you trace around, there are a lot of asymmetric connections. 3 Shires consistently hears Alban Hill better than Alban Hill hears 3 Shires. Or is 3 Shires low on receive? It's complex.

messages.author: Steve ● 7acc

messages.content_html: Stick something up the Wrekin, there's a MT node up there somewhere which is apparently working well

messages.author: bayjayzus
messages.text: There used to be a pirate radio station transmitting from the Wrekin years back so I think the authorities are quite twitchy about who is doing what up there, apparently they were even running a crafty power line from the TV broadcast building!. The other issue with the Wrekin is that Vodafone have recently been given planning permission for a 5G transmitter and there are also O2 and Three transmitters in the vicinity along with microwave and the elephant in the room a 64m digital TV mast, so there's lots of potential for interfence if you get it wrong. Looking at the Meshtastic map the Wrekin node appears to be on the NW of the Wrekin, quite a distance from & 20m or so lower than the peak, its targeting a node in Stafford. Looking at the Meshcore coverage tool there would be very little coverage to the South and East of the Wrekin (i.e me) without the use of several more repeaters to fill the gaps which appears to be what Meshtastic does in Telford where its coverage is actually pretty good, so it's nice to know it's doable. I'm doing some coverage tests as there are a few areas closer to me that have a c. 200m elevation but I still haven't found one with a decent line of sight to home there's old pit mounds everywhere around here getting in the way. I'm quite jealous of those in the S.E. they've got it relatively easy in comparison!.

messages.author: bayjayzus
messages.text: So I went out with the dog on New Year's Day to have a nose for suitable locations to place my repeater. There's a pit mound just over the road that is about 155m amsl which is only 15m or so higher than my house but for the first time in a month I actually got responses on my T1000e and from as far away as Oldham to the Isle of Wight!.. So I'm guessing I was actually on the main mesh?. Weirdly though I was in Shrewsbury today, there's supposed to be a couple of Meshcore repeaters there but I got absolutely nothing, Shropshire really is a black hole for Meshcore atm.. anyway I'm now looking out for a suitably tall tree on that mound and somebody able-bodied enough to mount my repeater high up for me.. Watch this space!

messages.author: Shaun M7HSA
messages.text: Hi all. Just stumbled accross this thread. Im based in Nuneaton and have a permanent repeater running in my loft using the heltec v4 and a diy yagi antenna pointing accross the town west towards Birmingham. It gives great coverage accross the town and using my portable node in can send messages back no problem. There are a couple of other repeaters in Nuneaton that are solar powered but i think they have gone offline recently as they only have small panels. Outside of Nuneaton we havent really picked much up unless out portable. There seems to be a bit of a gap between Coventry and Leicester and it would be great to see something good bridge the gap. Im also admin on the Facebook page UK midlands meshtastic and meschcore. Lots of new members popping up each day. Shaun M7HSA

messages.author: Matt
messages.text: Meshtastic is a Mesh, MeshCore is an infrastructure Both have their merits and drawbacks. If it’s distance you want to achieve MC wins hands down. If it’s a post apocalyptic grid down scenario, where nodes randomly appear and drop out, MT is probably the best option. All down to your use case, but unless you have a spare module in an EMP proof case, if SHTF it’s all pretty useless if that scenario ever occurs.

messages.author: princess
messages.text: from what i tested in bham, there is only my repeater in Selly Oak(hears Clee Hill), and another in Longbridge, that cover useful area. The latter doesn’t seem to link to the wider mesh, but got good ears. I currently have temp diy hw, that is old esp32 and ebyte 1w module, but those are quite deaf on rx. The storm last week had too much static and fried both my heltec v4s. Getting with a compromised rubber duck antenna is possible, heltec v4 , outside on the window sill, second floor in Droiwitch, i have stable link to Clee Hill South. But repeater needs to have ears. I have tried meshtastic, seems to have an easy link at home, but the app is so confusing, you can’t tell what node is RF, what is internet. Saying hello on the public channel - nothing. Meshcore has a bit of a charm and mistery, like making an impossible wspr contact to europe on 4m in the summer.

messages.author: mikeyp
messages.text: Morning all. Ok. I can now talk with @redmozzy ok. He could do with his antenna being external but that's ok. I think my repeater is ok. I think, having slept on it, there are a couple of factors at play here. We both live in a bowl. There is relatively little noise and interference and having a high gain antenna, we're able to hear things broadcast from a way away, hence picking up Bardon Hill. That said, Bardon Hill is high up, noisier and may not be able to hear my repeater over everything else. Also, I'm so low to sea level where I am, that despite elevating my repeater as much as I possibly can, I still don't quite have line of sight of the Bardon Hill repeater.

messages.author: bayjayzus
messages.text: I've been trying to run my Pig on the Wall repeater on multibyte as apparently I have a (very weak) mutual link to the main mesh through Clee Hill East which is also multibyte. It's not going well TBH I've barely been able to get any messages out in days Meshrank is showing no message activity from me either) I'm seriously thinking about reverting back. Maybe its early days for multibyte, it will probably work better when 1.15 is released? (edited)

messages.text: Yeah, it's been up and down for testing as I wanted to make sure the batteries could hold charge and now it's in its final position.. I've been speaking to a chap who has the Westdale Rak Repeater and doing some testing but it's so hit and miss. He could ping mine every now and again but I couldn't get out to reply. I've just been in Arnold with the companion as I wanted to stop and see where I could ping my own repeater. Basically it stops around Gunthorpe so i might need to speak to a few people in between to see if they'd allow me to place a repeater on their property.
